source_alignment.subject: English
source_alignment.source_statement: Segment spoken words into phonemes and represent these by graphemes, spelling some correctly.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack develops oral segmenting and sound-to-grapheme selection using low-text sound boxes. All pure-phoneme playback remains blocked until produced assets receive SSP-specialist review.

objective.child_goal: I can stretch a word, hear each sound and build it in order.
objective.statement: Segment simple spoken words into ordered phonemes and select known graphemes to represent those sounds.
misconceptions: Says the whole word again instead of separating its phonemes.
Hears the first and last phonemes but omits or changes the medial vowel.
Segments by letter names, syllables or onset-rime instead of the target phonemes.
Hears the phonemes but places graphemes in the wrong order.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A whole thing we say, like sun.
vocabulary.audio_script: A word is the whole thing we say before we stretch it.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: One picture card resting above three empty sound boxes.

vocabulary.child_explanation: One sound we can hear in a word.
vocabulary.audio_script: A phoneme is one sound in a spoken word.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: One glowing sound counter in one sound box.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Stretch a word and split it into its sounds.
vocabulary.audio_script: Segment means say the sounds in order, one by one.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A whole-word ribbon opening into three connected sound boxes.

vocabulary.child_explanation: One box for each sound we hear.
vocabulary.audio_script: Push one counter into a box for each sound.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Three large boxes with one counter space in each.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Say the word, then tap your ear to listen closely.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Establish attention to a spoken word without print or picture guessing.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Say the whole word once. Now get ready to listen inside it.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A familiar object picture, a mouth icon and an ear icon with no graphemes shown.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Stretch the word and give each sound its own carriage.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce segmenting as ordered phonemes within one whole spoken word.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The word is sun. Stretch it slowly and listen for each sound.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A picture of a sun above three empty train carriages used as sound boxes.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Watch one counter move for each sound in sat.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model oral segmenting before grapheme selection, with explicit attention to the middle vowel.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Say sat. Stretch it. I hear three sounds. One counter goes in each box.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three sound boxes fill left to right; grapheme tiles are revealed only after oral counting.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Push a counter for each sound, then choose the matching letters.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Link oral phoneme order to grapheme selection without handwriting demand.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Listen first. Push one counter per sound. Then build the word in the same order.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three large sound boxes above a small set of known grapheme tiles.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which counter is the middle sound in map?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check that all phonemes, especially the medial vowel, are represented.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Stretch the word. Touch each counter. Which sound comes between the first and last?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three counters with the centre position outlined, plus replay and adult-recorded word audio.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Nixi how to hear every sound in dog.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Confirm a repeatable say-stretch-push-check process through explanation.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I heard the first and last sounds. How do I find the sound in the middle?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Nixi shows two counters with a visible empty middle sound box.

manipulatives.purpose: Externalise one-to-one phoneme counting and preserve left-to-right order before grapheme selection.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Children tap, drag, keyboard-step or switch-scan one counter into each box, then replace counters with grapheme tiles.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Large targets, automatic snap, single-tap placement, no timer, optional adult mouth-view video and no requirement to speak into a microphone.

manipulatives.purpose: Provide a consistent say-stretch-segment routine with produced whole-word audio and gated phoneme replay.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Whole-word playback is separate from individual phoneme buttons; phoneme controls remain unavailable until their named assets pass SSP review.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: No browser TTS fallback, visual playback state, captions for adults, volume control and an adult-led mode for children who do not speak on demand.

practice.interleaving_notes: Sequence words strictly through the adopted SSP progression. Begin with oral segmenting, add grapheme tiles only for taught correspondences, and introduce adjacent consonants only after simple CVC segmentation is secure.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Count the phonemes in familiar CVC words before any grapheme choice is shown.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: produced whole-word audio + familiar picture after listening + 2-4 sound-counter choices
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Words and pronunciation must match SSP progression; pure phoneme buttons stay disabled until specialist-approved assets exist.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Segment simple CVC words into ordered sound counters with emphasis on hearing the medial vowel.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: whole-word audio + three sound boxes + one counter per heard phoneme + optional adult-led oral response
variant_blueprints.review_notes: No automatic speech scoring; teacher/parent observation and interaction evidence remain separate.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Select and order known graphemes after the spoken word has been segmented.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: three sound boxes + taught grapheme set + two plausible distractors + tap/keyboard/switch build
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Every target and distractor must be checked against the school's SSP teaching sequence before pilot.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Compare words differing in one phoneme and identify whether the changed sound is first, middle or last.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two produced whole-word recordings + sound-box comparison + position choice
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Minimal pairs require phonics-teacher review for accent, vocabulary familiarity and unambiguous pronunciation.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Retrieve previously taught segmentation patterns across spaced review without introducing untaught GPCs.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: SSP-tagged review queue + whole-word audio + sound boxes + known grapheme tiles
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Exclude browser TTS, untaught correspondences, speed scoring and handwriting-only responses.

body.prompt: Listen to the whole word. How many sounds can you hear?

expected_answer.observation: Child places or indicates one counter for each of the three phonemes.

hints: Push one counter each time your mouth makes a new sound.
question_variants.explanation: Sun has three phonemes, so it needs three sound counters.

body.prompt: Listen to map. Push one counter for each sound, then tap the middle box.

hints: Touch the counters from left to right.
The middle sound sits between the first and last sounds.
question_variants.explanation: Map has three ordered phonemes. The vowel phoneme is represented by the middle sound box.

body.prompt: Listen, segment the word, then place one letter tile in each sound box.

hints: Build from the first sound to the last sound.
Check that the middle box has the middle sound.
question_variants.explanation: The phonemes in dog are represented in order by d, o and g.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Says the whole word again instead of separating its phonemes.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child repeats the word once but places only one counter or gives no sound-by-sound response.
repair_sequence: Replay the produced whole word once without showing letters.
Model stretching the word while moving one neutral counter per phoneme.
Ask the child to copy the counter moves for a new familiar word.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child represents each phoneme in a new CVC word with a separate ordered counter.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Hears the first and last phonemes but omits or changes the medial vowel.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Only two counters are placed or the middle grapheme does not match the taught vowel phoneme.
repair_sequence: Show three sound boxes with the middle box outlined.
Replay the whole word and elongate only where phonically appropriate under SSP guidance.
Compare two teacher-approved words that differ only in the medial vowel.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child includes and correctly positions the medial vowel in two new CVC words.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Segments by letter names, syllables or onset-rime instead of the target phonemes.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child gives letter names, two chunks or an incorrect number of counters for a simple CVC word.
repair_sequence: Return to mouth movement and neutral counters with graphemes hidden.
Use one slow whole-word model and one counter per phoneme.
Reveal graphemes only after the oral count is stable.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child gives the correct phoneme count without using letter names.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Hears the phonemes but places graphemes in the wrong order.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The selected graphemes are correct but their left-to-right sequence does not match the spoken word.
repair_sequence: Keep counters in the original sound boxes.
Touch boxes left to right while replaying the produced whole word.
Replace one counter at a time with its taught grapheme.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child builds a new word in correct phoneme-grapheme order.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use a plain sound-box tray, quiet confirmation tones, no bouncing letters and no background music beneath speech.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace moving counters and trains with static box highlighting in left-to-right order.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Use produced, human-listened whole-word audio. Pure phoneme audio is unavailable until SSP-specialist approval; browser TTS is never a fallback.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Hide graphemes during oral segmenting, reveal only taught tiles at build time and keep written directions to one short line.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Use the same say-stretch-push-check routine, one target word per screen and no decorative competing motion.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow unlimited whole-word replay, extra pause time, slower adult-led modelling and a repeat-with-counters option.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Accept non-verbal counter placement, adult-observed oral responses and retries without recording the child's voice.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Use familiar pictured vocabulary, confirm the child knows the word meaning first and avoid treating accent differences as phonics errors.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires accurate oral segmentation and ordered sound-box representation for SSP-taught simple words; secure requires retained grapheme selection across spaced review with no untaught correspondences.
evidence.parent_summary: Can stretch familiar spoken words, hear the sounds in order and use known letter tiles to build them.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Segments SSP-aligned words into phonemes, preserves the medial vowel and maps taught graphemes in order using speech or accessible non-verbal responses.
next_actions: Revisit any unstable phoneme-grapheme correspondence within the adopted SSP sequence.
Move from counters to grapheme tiles only when oral segmenting is secure.
Offer handwriting practice separately so motor difficulty does not mask phonics knowledge.

qa.notes: Deep review sample for the first balanced Year 1 wave. Release is explicitly blocked pending school SSP progression mapping, produced whole-word and pure-phoneme assets, SSP-specialist listening approval, independent teacher review, SEND testing and child usability evidence. Browser TTS is prohibited.
